source: store.steampowered.com + fifplay.com + fifauteam.com + konami.com + efootball.codashop.com + store.playstation.com + lootbar.gg + gamingonphone.com + efootballarena.blog
notes: eFootball (formerly PES) is Konami's F2P football game. eFootball Coins are the premium currency. Pricing is regional and varies by platform (Steam, PS Store, Xbox Store, mobile app stores, Codashop). The tiers below are from the Steam item store (PC). Console and mobile pricing may differ slightly. eFootball versions update annually (2024, 2025, 2026) but the coin system carries over. Prices confirmed from Steam store listings and third-party aggregators.

coins_purchase_tiers.source: Steam item store (store.steampowered.com/itemstore/1665460/), PlayStation Store product listings, third-party price aggregators

tiers.bonus_note: 20 extra coins vs buying 5x100 (500 coins for $4.95)
150 extra coins vs buying at 100-pack rate
300 extra coins vs buying at 100-pack rate
800 extra coins vs buying at 100-pack rate
2000 extra coins vs buying at 100-pack rate (10000 coins for $99)

discount_progression.note: Higher tiers give progressively better value per coin

gbp.note: Only two GBP data points found via web search. Full GBP tier list not retrievable without direct store access.

eur.note: EUR pricing confirmed to exist on Steam and PS Store but specific per-tier values were not retrievable via web search. Pricing is region-dependent.
eur.estimated_note: Steam EUR pricing typically mirrors USD at 1:1 for major titles, so EUR tiers likely match USD values.

brl.note: BRL pricing confirmed to exist but not retrievable. eFootball uses regional pricing on all platforms.

sek.note: SEK pricing not found. Likely follows EUR pricing in the Steam store.

item_pricing_in_coins.note: eFootball monetisation centres on player acquisition through Chance Deals (gacha/box draw system). Unlike cosmetic-only games, coins buy gameplay-affecting player cards.

standard_chance_deal_single.description: Single draw from a Special Player List (box draw of ~150 players)

standard_chance_deal_10x.description: 10 draws at once (1 free draw included, saving 100 coins)

dream_team_power_pack.description: Dream Team Power Pack featuring Big Time and Highlight cards

national_team_pack_11_highlight.description: National Team Pack with 11 highlight player cards

pity_system.description: After 20 unsuccessful Epic Chance Deals, receive one random Epic player as Objective Reward
pity_system.note: Guarantees an Epic within 2000 coins worst case

real_money_equivalents.note: Real money cost depends on which coin pack tier was purchased

player_card_types.note: Base versions of all players, obtainable without coins
Improved stats vs standard versions, can be further developed
Based on standout season performances, unique card designs. Highest tier.

economy_notes: Dual currency: GP (free, earned through gameplay) and Coins (premium, purchased)
Unlike Fortnite/Valorant/Rocket League, eFootball coins buy GAMEPLAY-AFFECTING content (player cards)
Gacha/box draw system creates uncertainty in what you get for your money
Pity system guarantees an Epic card within 20 draws (2000 coins / ~$17-$20)
10x draw bundles offer a 10% discount (pay for 9, get 10)
Standard players are obtainable for free with GP - coins are for premium/Epic/Legendary cards
7 coin tiers from $0.99 to $99.99 - more granular than most competitors
16% savings at the top tier vs smallest pack - moderate volume discount
Available on Steam (unlike Rocket League post-2020), PS, Xbox, and mobile
Mobile versions may have slightly different pricing through app store IAP
Konami uses Codashop as an official third-party purchase channel
Coin prices can change between annual eFootball versions (2024, 2025, 2026)
